evals.description: A Java Spring Boot REST API project with no CHANGELOG and minimal docs. Skill should recommend changelog, document-project, and find-breaking-rest-api as high-relevance skills.

assertions.text: At least one recommendation includes a specific reason referencing something observed in the repo (e.g. REST endpoints, missing CHANGELOG)
The report includes /plugin install commands for recommended skills
The report does not mention performing a web or online search (online mode was not requested)

evals.prompt: Find skills for this project, search online too
evals.description: A Python FastAPI project. Skill should perform an online search in addition to checking local marketplaces.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-fs-1 && cd /tmp/eval-fs-1 && git init && printf '[project]
name = "myservice"
version = "0.1.0"
dependencies = ["fastapi", "uvicorn"]
' > pyproject.toml && printf 'from fastapi import FastAPI
app = FastAPI()
@app.get("/items")
def list_items(): return []
' > main.py && git add . && git commit -m 'init'

assertions.text: The report mentions that an online or web search was performed
The report includes a 'Marketplaces Searched' section listing at least one marketplace
The report includes at least one skill recommendation

evals.description: A well-maintained project that already has CHANGELOG.md, docs/, and no REST API. Fewer skills should be recommended, and the report should honestly reflect this.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-fs-2/docs && cd /tmp/eval-fs-2 && git init && printf '{"name":"mytool","version":"2.0.0","description":"A CLI utility"}' > package.json && printf '# mytool

A CLI utility.

## Quick Start

```bash
npm install -g mytool
```

## Docs

- [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md)
' > README.md && printf '# Getting Started

1. Install: `npm i -g mytool`
2. Run: `mytool --help`
' > docs/getting-started.md && printf '# Changelog

## 2.0.0
- Initial release
' > CHANGELOG.md && git add . && git commit -m 'init'

assertions.text: The 'changelog' skill is NOT recommended as High relevance (CHANGELOG.md already exists)
The 'document-project' skill is NOT recommended as High relevance (docs already exist)
The report includes a 'Skills Checked but Not Recommended' section explaining why some skills were skipped

evals.prompt: Find relevant skills for my repo
evals.description: A Go project with significant logging spread across many files and no dead-code tooling run. Skill should recommend improve-logging and potentially find-dead-code.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-fs-3/internal/service && cd /tmp/eval-fs-3 && git init && printf 'module example.com/myapp
go 1.21
' > go.mod && printf 'package main
import "log"
func main() { log.Println("starting") }
' > main.go && for i in $(seq 1 6); do printf 'package service
import "log"
func Do%d() { log.Printf("doing %d") }
' $i $i > internal/service/svc${i}.go; done && git add . && git commit -m 'init'

assertions.text: The report includes a /plugin install command for at least one recommended skill
The report lists 'easier-life-skills' in the Marketplaces Searched section

evals.description: A brand-new empty project with only a git init. Skill should handle gracefully and not crash, returning a report with whatever it can determine.

assertions.text: A recommendation report is produced (does not crash or produce an empty response)
The report does not recommend REST-specific or language-specific skills as High relevance when no language or framework was detected
